title: CloudFront Cache Keys, Origin Requests, TTLs, and Invalidation · Goroutine Forensics
summary: An incident packet contains a plausible cache-key, forwarding, and freshness contract with one identity, byte, checksum, policy, cache, ownership, cancellation, cleanup, or recovery defect; preserve the symptom and isolate first cause before repair. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in cache-key, forwarding, and freshness contract and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal d…
objectives: Include only representation-changing path, query, header, cookie, encoding, and authorization dimensions in the cache key.
Use origin request policy for additional required viewer data without unnecessary cache fragmentation or trust escalation.
Combine origin Cache-Control, minimum, default, maximum, stale behavior, validators, and error caching with content-change and privacy requirements.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Inspect the failure record · Debug Cache Key Identity
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug Go file-delivery team handles case 6597 during an SDK and infrastructure canary. Build deterministic Go evidence to align cache identity, origin forwarding, TTL, versioned names, and urgent invalidation; keep browser execution deterministic and move AWS claims to an authorized transfer gate; then change region, range, or signed header and explain the exact failure boundary. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Include only representation-changing path, query, header, cookie, encoding, and authorization dimensions in the cache key.

Inspect the Cache Key Identity trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and Go Inspect the failure record · Debug Cache Key Identity.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for include only representation-changing path, query, header, cookie, encoding, and authorization dimensions in the cache key. For S3, CloudFront, and Go Inspect the failure record · Debug Cache Key Identity, keep Cache Key Identity connected to executable Go storage and delivery decision evidence.

At S3, CloudFront, and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Compression Vary, trace this concrete input and predict runtime validation, authorization, custody, integrity, range, stream, cache, retry, cost, cleanup, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A debug Go file-delivery team handles case 1028 during a cost-anomaly investigation. Build deterministic Go evidence to align cache identity, origin forwarding, TTL, versioned names, and urgent invalidation; record cleanup, rollback, restoration, and residual-risk ownership; then change region, range, or signed header and explain the exact failure boundary. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Configure Brotli or gzip variants, Accept-Encoding cache behavior, already-compressed media, content length, and changed client capability evidence.

For S3, CloudFront, and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Compression Vary, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, file, object, policy, transfer, integrity, cache, fault, cost, restore, and controlled AWS transfer evidence: CloudFront compression reduces every file type and never changes cache identity.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Publish the incident record · Debug Compression Vary
steps.instruction:
Write the debug evidence note for Compression Vary: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A debug Go file-delivery team handles case 2647 during a compliance restore drill. Build deterministic Go evidence to align cache identity, origin forwarding, TTL, versioned names, and urgent invalidation; do not treat an ETag, CORS result, emulator, or successful SDK call as stronger proof than it is; then cancel halfway through and prove every body, part, and goroutine reaches a terminal state. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Configure Brotli or gzip variants, Accept-Encoding cache behavior, already-compressed media, content length, and changed client capability evidence.

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and Go Publish the incident record · Debug Compression Vary.
steps.why: Retrieving Compression Vary through the concrete cache-key, forwarding, and freshness contract case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For S3, CloudFront, and Go Publish the incident record · Debug Compression Vary, keep Compression Vary connected to executable Go storage and delivery decision evidence.
